author: HEMAD (Class 12)
title: hemadclass12/2024-05-21
html:
Huddle Notes May 21, 2024 by HEMAD (Class 24)

​

I just began the #100Devs course, not sure if this is addressed later in the course, but how do I know what is worthy of being added to Anki?

Do as much as you can, you’re gonna write trash cards

Needs to be your deck, part of the learning is making your deck

You'll gradually weed out ones that don’t work

What are some important questions to ask when you receive a job offer?

Base salary

What does bonus structure

Yearly review

What's the team like

Ask A LOT of questions, anything is fair game

Whatever impacts you the most

*a lot are in the bank to go over

Alexandria asks: good news first client, her mother. She wants a landing page and email list. She wants to know when negotiating with family

Family and friends pay double. Its usually a lot more work and treat it like ANY other client/job.

Ballpark budget

Proposal

EVERYTHING written down and agreed upon up front, if they don't agree don't take the job

Always make sure things are clear.

Little_data asks: what is Leon’s perspective? What were some more difficult engineering problems?

People. Its always people. [he pivoted to make sure this huddle is more about ‘the hunt’ questions, offered answering other questions like this via modmail]

​

Legend (ming) 100 hour project is working but the backend is really messy. What should he be focusing on right now?

making it less messy, also doing things to increase chances for multiple, many jobs/opportunities. Polish is for recruiters that don't know any better. You could have garbage code, but as long as you could talk about why it's garbage, that would be okay.

​

A recruiter reached out for a job but have been changing the date of the interview many times. Seems like they dont have organization. Should I interview there?

Yes always interview

​

how easy is it to find a fully remote role (UK/EU based) as a jr dev?Should I give up on it?It's the only way I can manage to work right now due to health stuff

its difficult, its possible but hard. Other 100devs folks have done it, but its A LOT of work

​

Is it important to not quit your current job until you find a new one as a software dev. Or should you take a risk and go all in ?

never quit if you can avoid it until you secure a swe job. When you are secure and stable and have money coming in and not worried about food on the table, guess what studyings a bit easier, going into interviews is…never quit your job until your new one

​

I have worked in the restaurant business for the past 20 years. I was just wondering what a typical day is like for a software engineer? Do you ever work late?

depends on who you are. Some colleagues RUN at 5 o’clock. Some never work late/weekend while others don’t seem to mind. Millions of swe’s, culture is different at different jobs. Find what you want. Is it better or worse hours? Typically way better, you tend to have more control over hours and time worked.

​

Is it productive to have a project before looking for coffee chat? I find that without a thing or project busy people refuse because it is poor use of time.

Dont delay start today. Often engineers like being supportive to those up and coming.

​

Can you remind us how we should respond to recruiters who reach out to us first but we don't have exp with the language they're hiring for ?

Depends on where you're at. Cant lose a job you don't have. If they're reaching out, who am i to not interview. Interview practice is interview practice, at least get that behavioral practice in. This is different if you already have a job, happily employed. At that point, may have a canned response involving absurd requests.

​

Should i have a camera for virtual coffee chat?

prefer camera on, even with first one. Leans toward respectful but wouldn’t think bad if not.

​

Is it advisable to ask in a coffee chat for them to suggest 3-5 or more people for me to network with?

just ask who else can I connect with. Every once and awhile had a coffee chat with a suit with manufactured questions. Don't mind that but not gonna be friends. Point is to make friends. If they come in treating it like an interview, that's when it should end. Avoid that ‘3-5 friends…’ sounds too transactional.

​

when you say get a client are we building websites for these clients? sorry I just started 100devs today 😅

YES. while completing 100devs, be tasked with getting a paid client. Helps when applying for opportunities.

Hey Leon, Are You doing any Resume review anytime soon ?

Not really, might do it again. ask on discord

​

I was talking to someone about 100devs & she was able to rec some1 to get in touch with &have a cofffee chat with and she is going to help me get an internship

Great that's why we network! Letsgo!

​

Should we give up on remote positions to be more competitive? Like focusing on hybrid and on-site positions?

Depending on the privilege of time dictates what job to go after. Prob go after the local job market first, then remote. Being local is competitive advantage

​

Started to prep the technical questions in the bank. The answers are easy to find but fully understanding the concepts is a tough one. Can you provide any tips?

Do the work. Put in time to learn the answers. Don't memorize as facts, they are guiding points for knowledge to do well in an interview. REal work is understanding the answers. Get WAY more at answering them on your own. Point is to understand them, ask on discord. Attend banki brunch.

​

should we cold call small companies for jobs?

Way more impactful things to do with your time. You can if you want.

​

Have you dealt with hard of hearing students/co-workers/clients? Any tips for people with this disability working at a SWE company? And maybe also for the hunt?

when you need accommodation, ask late in interview. Its about working with the company to get what you need to do the job. Specifically hard of hearing, try reddit communities or here on discord

​

I thought you recommended not working with friends as clients (due to scope creep).. were you saying you'd recommend it as a 1st client to allow leniency?

Yeah. Don't think those are contradictory. Friends and family, charge double and have a VERY clear scope. Charging double could be time (not just money), too. Take longer to do the project, if needed.

​

should we let them know about our ADHD if it helps us get extra time etc?

Ask for it at the latest point in interview process. Asking before even a phone screen is bad idea.

​

How do you suggest pitching ongoing maintenance to a freelance client for a website I've been working on for way too long (they keep requesting edits)?

Stop doing work for free! Create a boundary.

​

Is using age an asset in networking like college graduate?

i think so, senior folks like helping younger. Although, age doesn't matter too much in networking

​

What do you think of all the bootcamps that are shutting down?

Good? Haha you can learn this stuff for free. A lot of these boot camps were vc funded large corporations

​

A company that interviews for react developer then when sign up they expect you to use next. Js, twalind. Css, Redux and Typescript in 3 days, is it worth it?

Yeah, if you have that skillset, right? Ramp of 3 days is wild, maybe misreading, but thats a pretty common skillset

​

I did most of an electrical engineering degree, stopping when Covid started. How do I frame that as a positive thing? Low level, but conveys my tech background

yeah, there you go, put it on resume

​

Ats score matter? [note taker here…he deleted the question just as I tried to copy it down]

Yeah, should be 100.

​

We spend a lot of time getting rid of the bootcamp smell but if an employer googles 100devs the first thing they see on the website is "bootcamp". Why?

The first thing doesn't matter. you networked your way in, talking to real human. The second [Google] result mentions our hiring, thats a good thing.

​

I'm going to start a bank based tech internship but I would also like to start 100dev course. Is this advisable since I'll be learning different things?

No. please focus on internship first, deep not wide once stable, maybe ome 100devs. Don't do both and def not off the jump

​

author: HEMAD (Class 12)
title: hemadclass12/2024-06-05
html:
The following notes come from HEMAD, thank you again for stepping up.

I'm having coffee chats with people that the company is hiring but by the time I meet with them the company is no longer taking applications. Any tips?

It's still ok to build those relationships and they'll have you in mind next time hiring. If it's significant, the local environment may just move quickly. No shame in applying while setting up coffee chats.

Is The Bank still a good resource to use for developers with one year of experience who are looking to switch jobs?

Probably even better for you, honestly. You may be expected to know a lot of those questions AND you'll have better answers for behavioral questions. 3-5 years revisit questions as you'll have that experience.

Finally getting in touch & replies with engineering managers/leads on LinkedIn . What's a good resource to generate good questions to ask them and build rapport

-On the Bank, six questions you should be asking during interviews, that's a good place to start.

-If beginning, build rapport, make friends, and get them to talk about themselves, their lives, and good things about their company.

-Read how to win friends and influence people

Don't come with a preset list of questions just reading through. Be natural.,

How do I get better at git/github? I am struggling

Quantify what you're struggling with, and ask for help here on Discord. The cmd line can be overwhelming, give yourself time to figure it out. Initially use the same six commands over and over then just google it.

High school graduate is doing 100devs and WGU, studies just a couple of hours, and spends the rest of day wasting time and on the phone, advice pls

Oh boy.

-is it a problem? Are you able to do the amount of study you want to do? If you're able to do both, that's a lot of time. very few people can study 12 hours a day. Probably not a good thing for most people. Focused, reinforced learning time is more impactful. The rest of the day, do what you want to do!

-seek help if you can't get the things done that you want to get done.

-look into setting the phone to grayscale, you’ll not want to use it

CAN I / SHOULD I have a selfie/headshot on my personal website? Are employers allowed to see what I look like in that capacity before hiring me?

People are going to look you up, like on Linked In. What does your local market dictate? do that. Do what you are comfortable with, some don't have the safety to put headshots online.

How do you know when you know enough to be employed at a regular 9-5 swe role? It's hard to know when you know enough. Is it API and backend? Is it frameworks?

If you've done 100devs you've done enough. It comes down to showing you can do it. Your portfolio of work, paid work, etc.

Just finished my portfolio, updating my Linkedin. How do I list 100 devs on my Linkedin & resume? As Work or Education?

It's up to you. This is a training program, some put it as education or as work. Did you get a paid client? If you got paid because of 100devs, Leon considers that work. It should be under work.

best 4 projects to get me hired? im fullstack dev

No such thing as ‘best 4 projects’. Mentions 100hours project that solves a need, that's all you need. Go into interviews with a real problem that you solved, the time you put into it, challenges you overcame.

Do I need to get paid to add to my resume or is it okay if I do free things for my friends (can't get paid outside of my normal work due to visa stuff)

Everyone's situation is different, even if free, should have a contract in place as you open yourself up to liabilities. Demand the BEST reference because work is free. Unsure if that works for a visa, not an employment lawyer.

-you want to get to a point where someone vouches for you as a swe

I'm driving 90 minutes each time for meetups and want to try to find more opportunities through virtual. Any advice for that that's been updated since cohort 02?

Use meetup.com, twitter, a lot are still virtual. Communities are big, too

It’s hard trying to set up a daily schedule m? Any help?

-Don't go into your days like an accident. At a minimum, ask what ONE THING are you going to do tomorrow. Stop avoiding it, ask the question. Answer it.

-once you do the one thing, for a week or two, you can believe you can do two, then three, etc.

-if you have real issues with your daily schedule, you're not gonna magically study 12 hours a day. Start with one thing and do it.

I want to start a local 100Devs "chapter" - like getting local POC together in my segregated city to go through material. Any advice on how to start/what to do?

It's not something we advocate. Don't need to be local, just go here on Discord and get people together.

Can we get a reference from you?

If you do Huntober, you get a letter from Leon

Reading a 100Devs members (scars) guide to networking. Step 1 is to remove linkedin connections that do not serve you. should i just remove those that never reply?

I don't know any data behind that. I don't know if that harms/helps. Need to see data on it but don't think it matters, should focus on other things LIKE NETWORKING.

How do i know when I know enough to not smell?

If you've completed 100devs, crafted a story, and gone through interviews. Have to understand there are people getting jobs with half the skill and competence but have the vibes and confidence.

-show them you have value

Some students get jobs in 4-5 months or finish the cohort less than 9 months. What are they doing that I am not?

-Sacrificing mental/physical health, may have the privilege of more time. This is something that you will do for the rest of life.

-Normal pathway is HS, then four years grad, then internship. Normal process is FIVE years. So, you're worried about taking 5 months..stop. If it takes you less than five years, you have WON. no debt, built more (freelancing), stop trying to shave off three months

-comparison is the thief of joy

I know age doesn’t matter but honestly does it really not? How old is too old to be entry level engineer

I have no evidence of older folks doing worse. The folks that are older typically do better, they usually have other experiences that add to it. Yes, you may run into bad people that treat you poorly, but that's rare. Optimize your life for the thousands that bring positivity and fortune your way.

-stop being mean to yourself, bring positivity to yourself.

Work full time with exhausting toxic surroundings. After work is always tired. Any recommendations on how to put coding agenda into the work day?

Do your learning before work.

how can i stop stressing while coding?,everytime i code or theres an error i shake my leg and sweating and mad and so burnout

Its worth it to talk to a professional if you have a barrier that gets in the way of something you want to do with your life. Coding should be fun, its problem solving. Work with the community, lean on people. Use and connect on the discord!

how do we do huntober?

Just a list of req as you do the program

Is 2hrs a day enough to learn 100Devs?

Some, yes..some no. less time a day only limits the job

A company wants me to take over their site, a few things they want changed. Should I push to make a brand new website or work with what they have?

That's up to you and their budget. Leon is a fan of the new site where they can control it all, but they may not have that budget. Are you comfortable with doing all that?

How would you talk about experience gaps when you've not got a client yet but contributed to open source?

Get a client, the best way to explain gaps. Talk about open-source contributions then.

Met 2 people at my first meetup and used the followup/sheet, but noticed that neither of them are active on Twitter anymore. Is that still a valuable resource?

Yes, just maybe not for those two people

How do you respond to a website client that’s dodging the “what’s your budget” question, and throws out fiver and friend discount buzzwords?

I say no to way more clients than yes, use compassion, and point toward Fiver

At what class number do you feel its best to start freelancing during the cohort?

Class 15 or so

If you can work anywhere in the US, what city would you recommend we network in to maximize opportunity? SFO? I'm in Canada (dual citizen) but pay here is low.

It's all different. Anything that has a large tech economy that's not the valley.

My only real job has been as a housekeeper and I have been taking classes at community college. How do I craft a story based on this with zero tech experience?

You ran a small company that did housekeeping, and kept hitting tech problems so decided to expand your engineering skillset, talk about a project related to that

Freelancing has been rough, the local location everything handles things via WhatsApp and nobody seems interested, is it possible to freelance remote?

Yes, it is possible but 100% harder.

why is freelancing remote 100% harder in your opinion

Hard to build trust. Local is a real person, can meet in person.

Suggestion on using work time to study?

Depending on your job, don't do it without approval.

author: HEMAD (Class 12)
title: hemadclass12/2024-06-07
html:
notes by HEMAD

What is the best way to remain positive when you have been on the hunt for almost 2 years and still getting no offers? Just recover from burn out, any tips 4 it

If done in less than 5 years, absolutely crushing it, BUT if you are actively hinting, doing everything 100devs ask..gotta be analytical have people look at your behavior answers, technical, etc..there could be a ‘smell’ somewhere.-

-do banki brunch

-mock interviews

-do all of the above anyone months into the hunt process

​

I am really scared that I'll never become a software developer in this current market. Any tips on how to stay positive? Recently just recovered from burn out.

-Get off reddit, twitter. Life will improve.

- Misery loves company, as soon as people say “in this market” but we just heard of FIFTEEN ppl getting jobs.

-Don't listen to the folks not being successful, who hang with same people. And when probed, find out they’re not doing anything.

-There’s less “i sneezed and got a job opportunity”. Its hard now, HAVE to do good everything: narrative, 100hrs project, github, networking, etc to put yourself ahead.

-the things that we put in our brain are going to have the most lasting effects on us

​

what kind of story can one craft if they have never had a job and only did a hobby project for 8-yrs but no work experience.

-You my friend were freelancing

-possibly it was an unsuccessful business and you're transitioning.

-tie your narrative and your story into your 100hours project

​

around what time of the day is the prime time to reach out to recruiters?

Its not really worthwhile to worry about this, as long as its business hours you should be good (Mon-Thu) don't send on a Friday.

​

If a senior dev makes a mistake, is it better for the safety of my job to leave it alone and mind my business or is there a way to respectfully bring it up?

-be careful, take notes from how to win friends and influence people, admonish privately not publicly. Reach out directly to them first. Gotta know youre team, some may be sensitive.

​

How do we ask members of the community for coffee chats? 😊

Just ask, but network outside of community more, for hunt specifically. Look in local community. Starting off look here. Virtual coffee too, but eventually shift gears to focus on local tech economy.

​

When I send Hit List messages to Engineering Managers asking for a coffee chat, many respond by telling me just to click apply. How best to respond?

-you can tell them that you did but would still love to chat

-messages may come off too”i need a job”

​

Is there anything wrong with confirming that you're a self taught developer at the interview when they ask? If I do say yes do you think thats a bootcamp smell?

It's definitely a bootcamp smell, up to you what to say. Typically we say “community-taught”, training programs. Some co have biases, so be careful

​

I've been referred to a company, and I want to apply, but I'm currently on lesson 33, so I haven't gotten to node.js or the 100hr project yet. What should I do?

-Let them know you are not open to opportunities now but looking in six months or so

-this is very common esp sharing stuff/setting up online presence

-offer to circle back when more ready

​

Currently junior level roles are really competitive and keep landing mid-senior level interviews. Ideally junior is better, how do I know which one is best 4 me

Why would junior be better if you can land higher and you can handle it? You can always go down, harder to go up.

​

I will be attending renderATL next week. This will be my first conference and I am not sure what to expect/do. Do you have any advice? Will you be attending?

Leon will be speaking, attend it at 9am! Render sends out a guide, def read those.

-Going to make friends, listen to great talks. Don’t be afraid to talk to new people, have lunch with them(don't eat by yourself).

-Reach out online if you see people going. Go to afterparties!

-Might be a small 100devs meet-up.

-look into volunteering at cons

-take time to recharge!

​

On Tuesday's huddle you mentioned possibly having news on Cohort 3, is there any information you can provide?

-Fifteen jobs last month! Don't make an excuse, don't delay start today! In the amount of time you waited, you'd be one of the fifteen!

​

How do you overcome the fear of moving to another job and dealing with the new work environment, and meeting expectations?

-Make sure expectations are very clear. Have a meeting with mgr right away. Talk about 3, 6, 9 month plan written down, shared with mgr. Then GO GET it done

-as for environment, go slow..read How to Win Friends and Influence People

-never eat alone, join in after work culture

-take good notes, keep a dev journal. Note down wins! Keep a record.

-dont wait until end-of-year to go over accomplishments, go over often

​

Many companies apply unit testing for their projects. When they look at my projects without it, is that already a red flag? My 100 hours project doesn't have it

-No. be surprised how many co don't use unit testing.

-codewars, etc you've done basic testing.

​

Upcoming Amazon interview on Monday. I have been working on prep questions and not able to code answers correctly. I feel completely unprepared recommendations?

-Who cares cant lose a job you don't have, go into it rocking it!

-relax over the weekend.

-if it goes horribly wrong, reapply in six months.

-specifically, KNOW amazon’s processes..they are documented and easy to find.

​

If you’re coming to 100devs from a professional background (ie accountant, lawyer), how do you recommend including (or not including) that kind of experience?

-Do include it, bridge engineer to your past via 100 hours project.

​

– Laura came up to the stage –

-Currently has job opportunity came up. Has a call with CTO on monday. Looking for help on how to phrase work done in current job to look good to CTO. Its php/wordpress. Not very challenging, very simple front-end. She's been filling in a design role, but not what she wants to do. Says her sister got her the recommendation.

-Leon: don't overthink it. Its a vibe check. Think of good talking points of your job, try to “come across as normal”. Dont appear to be “sweating” the interview, be confident.

-this type of interview seems to be just a vibe check, they trust her sister to talk to Laura, so this is probably just that. Have a simple project to talk about your strengths.

-also talk to your sister again to get info on what to talk about, what to be prepared for. Take advantage of that privilege.

​

Do you have any recommendations for keeping structure in a gig based highly on call work environment?

-gig based: assuming, on demand work? That work is rough. Maybe depends on clients, on call schedule would be helpful. Find someone else that does similar work to pass work to them when get work outside of your schedule.

-keep 12 hour windows and referrals.

Mayanwolfe (Claire) added:

Not having set work hours and 24/7 on call is rough... been there in the past, never doing it again. It literally broke me, mentally

​

I am having a hard time coming with a 100 hours project idea even for struggles. Any tips?

-Pick something you hate or love. Or find a problem others have that you can fix. Ideally a problem you have to solve to add to your story.

-need to find that something to help bridge story and project.

​

I am on a government support for unemployment however they just want me to get any job to survive. Should I just get any job whilst still looking for a Dev role?

Yes. always have money coming in! Survive and live to study another day!

​

I am a huge kpop fan would doing a 100devs project based on that be too childish?

-No, not childish but would it be professional? If worried, maybe it is not professional. BUT seen people build what they love to build, so build it…so if you have a interview with another fan..BAM you’re in.

-example: weed delivery program, maybe doesn't work for interview, switched it to a coffee app

​

is 2 full stack projects is enough? to apply?

One is enough! Ideally more, but really just one good project is enough. Talk about what you built and learned.

​

How do you stand out when you're against CS grads that have internship experience? They say internship has more significance over anything else than projects.

You have the same thing: freelancing experience. Also, most CS grads don't have that experience. Don't listen to online chatter, don't compare to min/max people that are rare. EVEN IF you are, you’re PAID freelance is comparable.

​

Does having a CS degree really make you stand out? My friend with one keeps saying I need some certs to derisk myself. Hence why I keep failing the technicals.

No, it does open some jobs to specific CS-req jobs. Has nothing to do with you failing technicals. Question that friend, careful that type of advice.

​

What are the essential parts when talking about your past experience and skill set? All I can talk about is my 100 hours projects and freelancing. That enough?

Thats a big part of it but what makes you unique? Why would they hire you over the ~100 candidates? Have something that sets you apart

​

What do you show when you have past work but you can only show your work and not the entire project? (WP Project)

-You talk about it, show as screenshot, don't need to link the entire project.

​

When talking pay, a recruiter said they're new to the role and are not sure what devs at this level make and they haven't told them the budget. What do I say?

Please talk to them and get back to me, thats it

​

Do we have any tea spills scheduled soon? Can we have some of the recent ppl who got jobs come back and share their good vibes & tips with us?

Hopefully soon

​

if I say I was freelancing for many years, wouldn't it be expected of me to apply for a senior position while I want an entry level job..how do I ask for entry?

Im interested in entry-level roles

​

My non-coding job that asked me to make a tool using Google Sheets for error reports, wrote some JS to automate parts of it. Can I leverage this for resume?

Yes, that should be on your resume, you did that

​

how to get loads of green boxes on git all my projects are set on private cause they are work related

You can include private on your heatmap. Turn on that setting

​

I'm in a data QC position, how do I convince my supervisor to let me have a set number of hours spent coding a way to automate my tasks?

Let them know you have those abilities. Ask to automate it, show your worth. Worthwhile to ask.

​

been doing 100devs for 1yr and 4 months. i am only on class 53 and i feel useless

Build something, that's all. You’re USEFUL. You’re putting in the work, asking questions, you’re doing more than the ones that dont get past class 1. You have skills and experience that others would love to have.

​

Why are there specific companies that only want candidate with a CS degree? Surely CS grads mainly study the theory and a build a few projects on their course.

Don't know how to hire, just a filter.

​

My full-time job pays peanuts, but it’s something. It seems like waste of time, and not relevant to my goals. Suggestions for networking into a better role?

Yes network into a better role

​

Any tips for someone who is a returning citizen, as far as reaching out for chats, explaining it on a resume, ect?

Dont. you just don't have to. It probably wont come up. Don't snitch on yourself. Reach out to folks here.

​

What are the people getting placements projects like? i feel like my own everything is rubbish and i might be too shit to do this

Gotta focus on the things you accomplished in the short amount of time you’ve been doing this!

​

Why we choose Codewars over Leetcode?

Leet kinda silly until you get better. Brute Forcing is better

​

Any tips for having a consistent study schedule?

Start super small. One victory a day, work up to more.

​

hobby project = blog but did have sponsorship a few times.

Thats a biz, not a hobby if got paid

​

two parter - so that job posting i have an interview with on monday. but it seems like it is 3-4 jobs in one. what are you thoughts on this and I am countering!

Doesn't matter, just get the offer!

​

Do you have to submit all HW to complete the cohort 2?

Yes, need it to be eligible for huntober..IF you want it

​

I feel like i remember either seeing/hearing a remark from Leon a while back about looking at alternatives to X for class sign ins- is this still a possibility?

Not yet. When cohort 3, may be alt then.

​

I feel like i remember either seeing/hearing a remark from Leon a while back about looking at alternatives to X for class sign ins- is this still a possibility?

Dont pay money to learn

​

is it okay to build stuff out side of what 100devs teaches like how 100devs teaches js can you make something in another language like php for the 100hrs project

Don't feel constrained to stick only with 100devs. Just complete a project then go to the next one.

​

Hi Leon almost all the jobs in my area require a CS degree If I'm unable to network this job do I hit apply anyway and what if recruiter asks me if I have one?

Apply anyway. If a real hurtle, enroll in community college. And say you're on the way to cs deg

​

got dc, wanted to know how do I ask for entry level job if I say I have many years of freelancing, when they would be expecting me to apply for senior position

Not abnormal to want to go to an entry level role.

​

author: HEMAD (Class 12)
title: hemadclass12/2024-06-18
html:
notes by HEMAD

1.how was the conference? Any professional beans to spill with the 100devs community? ◦ so many of us there! photo in ?📝follow-along-materials ◦ people from 100devs two years ago showed up! ◦ cool to see how deep our professional network really is!

2.Advice on finding more clients? I have been strictly cold calling. Any out of the box ideas? ◦ cold calling works in certain markets ◦ start locally, show up. go to any small business in your area that looks like need help online presence. face to face works, you're more trustworthy. ◦ make sure you have solid website/online presence and examples, also a testimonial or reference ◦ maybe lawn signs? like political campaigns? so many ways to grow freelancing biz once you have everything in order ◦ do you have the ability to close? if not, work on that. ◦ do things that dont scale, use revenue from that to do the things that do scale 3.Advice to someone on the hunt for 8 months? I feel like everything except job hunting is a waste of time (ex: coding, freelancing, meetups, etc) Thoughts? ◦ once past three months range,time to be analytical of yourself, what can you improve, your gaps and work on fixing them. ◦ If you cant identity, work with someone that can help point these things out ◦ most common: not talking highly enough about self ◦ talk about yourself as a real professional, build yourself up, carry that energy into the hunt ◦ still need to code challenge, contribute to projects to keep skills fresh ◦ one example of alumni: conferences worked better than linkedin for them. Switch things up if you’re finding things you are doing aren't working ◦ REMEMBER: if you do it less than five years, you’re way up. You’re still doing a shortcut! from Mayanwolfe (Claire): It can be helpful to get a second option on your job hunting approach, because if you've been at it for a while you'll be more likely to beat yourself up rather than looking at things from an analytical perspective. From chat: I'm asking this as an introvert! How can I get connected to people? ◦ read THE book How To Win Friends and Influence People ◦ just start. We all suck at the beginning. ◦ bring a friend at first, join spaces that work for you to build to other spaces ◦ anxiety responses that get in the way seek professional help! ◦ networking doesn't have to be local meetup in person, twitter works, discord. Find them that works for you! -noone cares about your social missteps more than yourself!! - Mayanwolfe

4.Tips on making a website for a family member? (freelance) probably not going to charge them. ◦ wrong path! Family pays DOUBLE! Always. Family is always gonna cost you the most energy, time, etc. if they care they’ll want to support you ◦ if they don't, why should they get it free if they're not supporting you! ◦ worst client you ever have most likely will be someone close to you. ◦ if you don't charge that’s really fine, its up to you. BUT: USE A CONTRACT: it stipulates what happens when something goes wrong. Easy to point at and review when conflict happens. ◦ if you ignore everything else, at least USE A CONTRACT ◦ tips on making the site: quick on revisions. Don’t ignore your normal process and treat them just like any other client. Stick to contract to make thing smoother

5.Someone has a potential client that want me to work on the project for them. We have a meeting set up to discuss it, what to bring up doing our meeting? ◦ great, its still the same process. ‘What's your ballpark budget’ give them a proposal at that budget. Demand the same deposit, time paid, etc. review how to freelance class. Side note: don't be afraid to barter for goods! Trade services for your work 6.Sorry, this probably gets asked so much but tips to deal with burn-out on the hunt? I cant get over the feeling that i don't want to hitlist anymore. ◦ its okay to take a break with a defined start/end date. Get loved ones on board with your break and have them check in ◦ burn happens because those in your life may not be bought in on your doing this. They don't realize the seriousness, that you're trying to better yours and their lives. Get them bought in, make sure they know how hard you're working, that way they support, encourage, which helps with burnout ◦ another symptom of burnout: thinking of long term long goals that are out of your control. If your goal is to get the job at the end of this journey you WILL burn out. Do the small things first. Become a better developer each day. ◦ when you meet and finish these small daily goals, celebrate them and with those around you! ◦ focus on the process, you will reach the goal**

From chat: I also like to do reflections, what worked and didn't work. Leon: yes! Journal your journey! Celebrate the work you did do. 7.Thoughts about the overemployed movement? Should I try having 2 dev jobs at the same time? ◦ yeah why not? If you can handle it. As long as you're bringing value to the company and not doing anything unethical. 8.Are there folks that have landed a job remotely in a first world country being from Central/Latin America? Could you do a tea spill with them? ◦ yeah of course. A lot of first jobs from 100devs were there. Did some teal spills in the past Leon says tea spill on Friday will be led by Marciac95! Marcia came up to stage: ◦ was a farmer, found 100devs in four and a half months couldnt open vscode to getting hired as python back end dev! ◦ on friday 6pm est she’ll give more details on the process 9.Starting my own local meetup called Techtastrophe in a small city in Virginia. Any tips to make it big and provide value to attendees so they come back monthly? ◦ don't worry about the size, big can be death of a community, focus on the connections, build rapport with those that show ◦ ask those that show why they came. What they wanted to get out of the meetup. Provide it! ◦ as you scale, over time worry about the size

10.What should we do if the salary for software engineer is bad compared to first world countries? They want one person that does everything an IT department does. ◦ sometimes the local economy does not support the job that others may elsewhere. ◦ may go after global hiring, but know that it is ten times harder to get! ◦ if leon was in this situation, hed make it his life goal to know every single person in every global hiring firm. Its a lot of hard. Work.

11.My client is ghosting me on payment. I didn't get a deposit or contract because they're a family friend. Should I still use them as a reference? ◦ NO. They wont say anything positive about you if they did that! ◦ you want a client that is so happy that they used you, they’ll talk so highly of you. A person ghosting you wont talk highly of you! ◦ CONTRACT CONTRACT CONTRACT. YOU MUST DO THIS NEXT TIME ◦ if payment is involved, ALWAYS get a deposit. Minimum 25%, then milestones. As you get that next payment, finish that portion of the work.

12.catchup crew here! Since there isn't a live cohort, how do we show that we've been actually going through the program? ◦ submit everything like normal. Check in on twitter, etc. use https://communitytaught.org/

author: HEMAD (Class 12)
title: hemadclass12/2024-06-21
html:
from HEMAD

here are some notes from tonight, june 21 2024. tune into the tea spill, i wont take notes for tha!

how to did practice and prep for your technical interview. every interview my peers that got were coding challenges and a white board PREP! Calms the nerves, this should be good 10-15min before interview,

I see many in the top 10/20 of the codewars clan, still without a job? Should we worry? Is it almost hopeless? no, codewars doesn't move the needle. Technically prep is just one door that you open. Dont put all your eggs in that basket, make sure you’re networking weekly! Dont just do the easy things!, a lot of folx are in that community but not in the hunt, keep that in mind,

I have been taken on board for a Hackathon sponsored by Google. The organizer said people will be head hunted after 4 days. How can I stand out as a participant best thing you could do: make sure you’re reaching out, you’re connecting with them afterward. Be persistent and follow up, take the initiative and things will eventually work in your favor,

I have weekly mock interviews with my senior dev friend. When its the real interviews I tend to feel nervous and anxious after being rejected many times.Tips? first: totally normal. Give yourself grace! It’s part of the process, just do more. With any company, any phone screen, get used to going into that environment, and once you’ve done some you’ll be used to it., know what you’re going into, actually ASK them what you’ll be tested on and more often than not, they'll tell you!, go into every interview with the mindset this person is your best friend and wants the best outta you. Just positive vibes for yourself to be even more confident!,

author: HEMAD (Class 12)
title: hemadclass12/2024-07-18
html:
notes by HEMAD

JULY 18 2024 SECRET HUDDLE NOTES Lesson 3 tech interviewer here, It's a 2.5hr JS interview. should I focus on practicing with Codewars and watching tutorial videos? Any video recs? pls help

Watch the js review class

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1sRJYuaqoiI&pp=ygUXMTAwZGV2cyBqcyBzdXBlciByZXZpZXc%3D

to learn the basics, yes practice codewars and take the shot! Learn from the interview process, improvements, changes. Beauti

How do I apply to do a tea spill?

Just send a modmail!

It's been my third attempt now in about a year and I'm struggling to keep up with the classes due to personal issues even when I've planned. Any advise please.

Give yourself grace. We’re all on our own journeys with different paces.

Build a plan that's realistic to your life. You need

What's your opinion on the current job market for Junior roles? Any advice regarding it? Ive been told itll take me 1-2 years to land a role due to the market.

Its going well, better than it has in a couple years. Check ?🎉celebrations channel!

1-2 years can be normal,it can vary for a lot of people. Some do it quicker, but the traditional route is about five years (uni/internship), If you get a job in 1-2 years you’ve achieved a shortcut!

layoffs are down, job postings are going up

The internship person here there are only 3 people in the company, it's a brand new startup is that bad?

Its not bad, just a very different experience. There may be NO process: no on-boarding, etc but you could learn a ton. It could go either way.

Just reached The Hunt in the program. Will there be a new hunt this October? Or should I just follow the huntober 2023 channel?

There will def be a new huntober, don't wait! Don't delay start today!

I'm at the hunt trying my best to get in touch with ppl in companies that I wanna join and even though I've tried to connect with many ppl I only got one chat

welcome to the game! If you’re using Linkedin connect, that could be a bad way. But that could be just me. My best platform is twitter and in person.

you should be sending dozens of messages over multiple platforms over spaced out time

Leon, you mentioned something about ADHD making us think we're boring in conversation, essentially self sabotaging. Can you elaborate more on that?

im not a doctor! Anecdotally, my experiences are my feelings on these things.

As for myself, folx with ADHD have trouble in convos because of the need to relate. Trying to connect with that person, but the connection is often the opposite of how you’re supposed to connect. Its just how my brain works. How to Win Friends and Influence People has really helped.

big advocate for therapy, talk to your doctor.

Mayanwolf: ADHD can make it difficult to listen actively without going off on a tangent or immediately trying to tie it to our own past experiences. I may have an offer at Salesforce. I have some developer friends saying it's a bad idea because it locks you in to their technology. Thoughts?

Working AT Salesforce is no different from joining any company. No worries there

Growth as an engineer should not come from your job for this very reason. You must always be training outside of your career to grow

I keep seeing that Software Development is a dying field that is going to be taken over by AI. What are your thoughts on this?

This is true, eventually and so will every other job. I don’t think its a quick as people think, maybe within ten years? By that point every major industry will be disrupted enough

At this point, theres no other field that you can learn for free and eventually get paid

if/when that happens you’ll have to move on anyway, take time now to learn

every company would still need people to integrate AI, be that person. Start/continue learning today

I've been networking hard in person the past few months but someone I networked with is a bit needy and an emotional vampire. How do I set boundaries? -You block them online. If in person, just say Hey i enjoyed this conversation, I’d like to take time to meet other folx and connect later! How do you respond to an interview offer after you were rejected for the job, that is, they contacted you to put you back into the running for the job? I've been going on some coffee chats. I'm struggling with feeling like I'm such a weird and awkward person that nobody will ever recommend me for a job. Help

image.gif failed to upload

not true. If focused on the job portion, you're doing it wrong. Coffee chats are to make friends. Just like any other time, sometimes that doesn’t work. Move on network and see who you meet. Try a different meetup, something else you’re interested in to start.

never eat alone, read HTWFAIP, talk here on discord

Just started a new dev job this week. How do I say "I don't even know what I don't know" when asked if I have questions without looking incompetent?

image.gif failed to upload

Very common question, ask your team and managers if its okay to ask lots of questions! Most will always say yes.

Make sure to be proactive when you get answers, make a spreadsheet of questions and answers, team will notice. Ask a specific person if they can be a point-person/on-boarding buddy to ask all silly questions during your beginning there.

make sure to bring them a coffee, or something else as an appreciation.

Any updates on the Colossal or other Agency jobs? The Agency Projects channel has been very quiet. Is there more information on another channel?

Nope, working on direct talent placement that introduced a lot of good and also new hiccups to deal with

Hey leon, I'm on the hunt, i have sent msgs to 60 companies, setting up chats via linkedin and email, and have had no luck, should I switch to events & x? Thts?

But how many questions total? Need to send more. Use different platforms

I cold called 600 people and no clients yet, tips?

maybe thats not working for you, a lot of ppl arent responsive to cold calling.

showing up in person goes a much longer way to the company

How do you respond to an interview offer after you were rejected for the job, that is, they contacted you to put you back into the running for the job?

This happens all the time. Not a bad sign, just got to the slightly better candidate

no feelings at all when they do reach back out to you, just accept the interview. Once get an offer, then can question the process

Suggestions for onboarding as a new dev? I know, "never eat alone, and document the process." Is there anything else I need 2 know 2 get up 2 speed/shine? Thx!

make sure talk with manager for expectations 3 6 9 etc months and how youre crushing those goals

How do we market as an overall engineer and not just web development? Say the position is not web development?

Youre a SWE, web is just something you know how to work in

A company that I interviewed with went with someone else. Is there a good format for a thank-you letter I can send back? Anything key that I should include?

Just thank for time, looking forward to a good fit

Code challenge given for interview. I have 30 minutes to complete. I know that is not enough time with my learning disability. Do I submit challenge incomplete?

Nope, ask for accommodation if you need it

How to study better? I'm very interested in being more effective. Thanks leon! Learning how to learn, put those in practice! As an experienced developer, can I contribute to Agency jobs? I don't mind doing it for free as long as it helps the community

image.gif failed to upload

Don't like free labor! Look into maintainers

Is DSA (Data Structures & Algo) required for Full stack engineer interviews?

For some, not all

How do you prepare for a second interview with the team but this time the CIO is included? Nothing much changes My work encourages me approve PRs, but I'm afraid I'm making their code base worse if I miss something. Advice? You don't have to do singular PRs, ask for a second set of eyes, ask team manager I have seen videos advising junior devs to focus on different stacks not the MERN, as most companies work with c# .net java, thoughts?

It doesn't matter. Learn basics, then learn what you need to for the job

Seems practical, but doesnt speak to the education. People don't often learn the code, yes you may need to learn that eventually,but start with the basics.

Stack doesn't matter, its can you learn, can you pick up the skills you need resume has 100devs & freelance employed mid 2022- pres. got a non-SWE job that is related enough to put on res. bad for resume to say curr. employed at 3 co’s?

That ends when you're employed elsewhere

what are good resources or what kind of work to grow as an engineer outside of your job to get better?

Building stuff

What is the best way to get feedback from a client regarding the entire process (consultation, contract, building, end product, etc)? I want to improve!

image.gif failed to upload

Just ask don't overthink it

author: Jess St Pierre
title: 2025-09-12: 100Devs Huddle Notes
html:
2025-09-12: 100Devs Huddle Notes

Written: By

Jess St-Pierre

You need to quickly start looking for jobs. Dont over-analyze!! You need to practice interviewing and stuff!-> Is my resume good?-> Am I getting callbacks? Start as soon as you can to get a feedback loop going!! Most people will be ready after completing 100hrs project if you’re doing 100Devs. Need to know what interviews look like to study for them!!

If someone criticizes my work not-constructively than I would communicate with them politely. Just explain how you want the person to constructively criticize, in private. Only if that person is a random colleague who wasn’t assigned to criticize you!

Interviewers probably wont look at your portfolio. Might look at resume and LinkedIn so dont waste too much time on the portfolio! Need to know what folks actually care about.

There are still plenty of remote opportunities! Depends where you’re located. Some are back in office and some are hybrid some are remote!

You kind of wanna have all your ducks in a row before you start taking all your time job searching, so you should have done your 100hrs project.

If you had an internship, it depends on your official title for it! You should be talking about the things you’ve done and the things you accomplished. Nothing smelly about a work experience! You always wanna let them dig if they wanna dig, there is no need to state every-single thing but never lie! Want them to formulate most grand opinion of me as possible. Dont snitch on yourself.

If diagnosed with ADHD = take Ritalin or Adderall, get that good stuff. Find a like-minded community. What works for me might not work for you! Work with a professional directly for strategies gonna be different for each person! Therapy / exercice / eating right! Or something like that.

Clients that want more but cant pay more. You respond with “hey this is a great change of scope, we would need a new contract / proposal for the work with a new payment.” (Not sure if Leon included the payment part so take with a grain of salt from me)

Very soon we have Huntober starting! The month for hiring until January. This year we will do it biiiig!

Can I use projects from class if I havent done 100hrs yet? Yes! But make it your own, dont just take the homework directly. Not for portfolio sake, so you have something to talk about = 100hr project!

At what point do you lower your quote if u dont get a reply from your client! = Never!! If anything you up it “hey I know we’ve been talking for a while but I’m starting to get booked this month. I could do it next month but for a higher price”

100hrs is to talk about during interviews, not simply to showcase on your portfolio!!

Reach out to 3 managers in 1 company, keep reaching out. Stop messaging them only when they tell you to stop. initial than 1 week than 1 month at least. And keep them in the loop. Click apply shouldn’t be the only thing you do! Reaching out is for every other role they also post afterwards not just this one.

Its been 2 years since I graduated. How do I stay motivated? Have to audit your process! What has to change/ improve? Could be anything! Take that in a systematic way! Be analytical not critical of yourself and your process. Bigger hitlist! More networking! More people in funnel so that the holes in it are obvious! Need feedback loop. Either not enough in funnel or big cracks in it!

How much wiggle room do companies have from initial offer? Depends on a lot of things! On average 10k to play with. Its such a hard question to answer! You have to figure out what the range is for each opportunity!

If it takes 2-3 yrs to finish studying web dev: network the whole time!!! Need to have so many people in your corner that would be willing to refer you!

For case study, can I redo client projects because the old are cringey?! Yes you can show the improvement you had over time!

Tips for ppl going to CYC alone! = You’re not going alone, 100Devs will be going! A lot of people are saying in the CYC channel that they are going and want to be your friend! Use that channel to meet with folks! See who’s going, follow the speakers on socials, make Twitter lists of people going! And then get a conference buddy, can say you have social anxiety and go with someone! The programming podcast also has a good ep on how to conference!

Networking and a little bit of freelancing! Kind of gotta do it all. Do freelancing, just for things to talk about during interviews. Networking is going to be the most helpful post-grad.

How add 100hr project to resume? = What value did you provide? What have you done that no one else did? What can a recruiter take and then brag to engineering manager about?

How to learn/ take notes? = As Im watching and learning I ask questions, note what I cant answer + goes into my Anki for review. No need to take notes on stuff you know already!

People can smell if you write your resume with AI, so dont do it!

By apply I mean network your way in! Talk to them build a relationship, the jobs will come!

author: Jess St Pierre
title: 19-09-2025: 100Devs Huddle Notes
html:
19-09-2025: 100Devs Huddle Notes

Written By:

Jess St-Pierre

Coarsera course went paid but there will be a work-around

When adding AI on my projects can I use googles cloud or Microsoft’s cloud without putting my billing info? = Azure used to not require a credit card, but I will check… In the past we had a way to do that, azure not having to have a credit card put in

I applied online and I said yes to having experience in AWS - you wanna have those keywords on your resume so that there is a match with what they care about, modify each resume and skip AI when using to do this. They can smell it. Network your way in. If you’re clicking and applying that’s different (without networking).

Any advice on how to get started with AWS? = they provide good docs and free certification program,

roadmap.sh

is a way to get started as well. Play with these tools, take an old project and spin it around one of these tools. Use limits to not make mistakes when using these cloud providers! Free limits! I tend not to use them that dont have limits!

To prepare my schedule with Huntober, do you have a start date? = Yes starts on one of my normal days, that Friday or Monday. Usually lasts around a month. 3rd or 6th that we start! We meet every day. Try to do data-structures and algorithms, practical advice around interviewing. How to get hired, tea-spills, and mix a lot of stuff into it to make it fun. Biggest tradition we do in 100Devs. Typically around normal time 5:30pm EDT and go half and hour usually. I’ll share more after CYC.

How can I be kinder to myself due to my slow learning progress? = Slow learning pace is totally okay!!! Its like comparing a F1 driver to a hiker. The hiker will observe more, will maintain more info. Even if the F1 driver will get there first. Difficulty grasping even simple concepts = they dont know how to learn, that’s okay because you can learn how to learn! Dont just throw yourself into a book and you dont read! If you throw yourself into a lecture and havent been in a lecture in 10 years you’re gonna struggle. You need the tools!! You need things like chunking and space-repetition, etc… you have to learn the tools first and than the learning can come after. Every single person who learned how to learn has become successful at learning! Everything changed! 8 hours a day of studying = LeBron levels of studying. There’s always going to be outliers, where people dont get tired and dont loose focus. Dont compare yourself to them!! It is a muscle you need to build it up over time but also dont compare yourself to LeBron! Got to give yourself grace when learning! Build up the muscle to keep at it for the long haul. Measure the years not the hours!

When freelancing, when do you add privacy policy and terms and conditions to websites? = Day 1, with chatGPT and stuff. Cause of the google web crawling.

Received my first interview on class 39… how do I prepare to not snitch on myself? = You’re gonna have lots and lots of interviews, some will go well and some will go horrible! Not entirely in your control, if you’ve done all the prep we talk about. Then its just a matter of being a fit or not! You wanna be getting out of the interview knowing what other interviews will look like: be analytical for this first one. What went well and what didn’t go well. You’re gonna get so much experience by going through the process. Nothing bad can come out of it, as long as you focus on improving. Look at the Bank, for interview questions example. You wanna have a really good narrative, why does it make sense to be the best candidate for this role and talk about certain projects. Look at the “how to interview” class! My biggest pet peeves in life if someone sending the interviewer an email saying “I know it didn’t go well”, when that’s not necessarily true, you just lost it by saying that. The other person could’ve been grumpy and hungry. Dont sell yourself sort. Talk your talk. No humbleness.

Is a startup without financial stuff a good job? = Its not a job, its a trap. Please do not work a job for no money. Some internships with big companies are worth it but not often. Its not a startup that’s trying to get free labor.

What is the 100Devs wisdom on those that can only work 15-20 hrs per week? = Freelancing is great for people without structured hours like parents and stuff. I highly advocate for freelancing. There are a small amount of part-time remote software engineering jobs, you really have to network your way in. Easiest = freelancing. But there are companies who hire for part-time.

How do you handle competitive at work? How to handle bullies? = It comes down to there’s levels to it and so its hard to give 1 size fits all. Read how to win friends and influence people, works with a lot of people. Can pull someone to the side and tell them you dont appreciate their behavior. If you’ve done all the stuff then talk to HR and people. Is competition part of the org or individual person? I would talk to the person if its the latter. Time to find another opportunity if its a company culture thing!

I completed 100Devs last year but didn’t submit any homework? = You dont need to submit anything! Doesn’t do too much for you than certify that you did the course. This is a jobs program so wont help you too much in getting a job.

Im from Canada and not willing to change countries for a while, should I network with fellow Canadians? = Yes!! Mostly you want to network with Canadians. I would make a list of companies and add to your list to network your way in and than find countries outside your country and make it a secondary hit-list. First you do the Canadian networking hitlist and than the American or NA one! Some USA companies hire remotely and anyone from NA so should still take that into consideration!

Thoughts on walking into offices with my Resume? Not from those cities ? = It could probs hurt more than it could help. I dont think that’s gonna be a good look, especially some have security now. Dont really work in tech offices. You wont get anywhere with it. What I would do however: Find every living being that is in the company and city and say : Hey I’ll be in NYC wanna do a coffee chat? Should also do meetups and networking while you’re there!

My resume has both 100Devs and professional sabbatical? = You need to have a good narrative for professional sabbatical. Ive seen people put it on there and seen people having trouble putting it in their narrative.

author: lets (class 63)(lurk)
title: letsclass63lurk/2025-10-16
html:
from lets(class 63)(lurk)

Come Learn How To Build The Perfect Resume! One that opens doors, gets interviews, and helps you land your next job!

You'll learn the mistakes that get your resume sent to the trashcan

Understand what recruiters actually care about

Walk away with a template that you can put into action ASAP

We need a couple sets of resumes – there are resumes that work really well for individuals who know how to finesse and a resume for those who lack that ability. They are very different resumes. For somebody who can articulate the things unsaid, to fill in the blanks of something that isn't explicitly stated – they can go forward with a different resume. Some people need their resume to be brutally honest.

You CANNOT use the text that comes out of resume generators because it smells like AI but it gives you STRUCTURE and DIRECTION.

A hiring manager is the person calling all the shots when it comes time to get a job. They have head count, probably write the job description and the final say in who gets hired. This is different from every single company but generally this is how it shakes down. Then you have genreally two other big folks outside of the hiring manager – recruiter and sourcer.

Recruiter coordinates all the inbound talent and filters for the hiring manager. Hiring manager should only ever look at a handful of candidates and only during an on-site or interviews. Sometimes the recruiters needs help for the inbound interest – so a sourcer.

Sourcer are in charge of getting viable candidates in front of the recruiter, they're the one who send the emails and linkedin messages.

Sometimes this process gets BIGGER. You're at a larger company and maybe you have multiple recruiter/sourcers and thus you have a coodinator that helps triage everything.

If you're going to network your way into the job, then hiring manager will have the most sway but recruiter/sourcer may be most visible. And know that they may be managing lots and lots of people

What does the process look like? 250 resumes submitted. All of them are run through an ATS (applicant tracking system). ATS is not AI, not robot, not somebody instantly rejecting you. A CV compiler gives you a score. There's no mystical match score and lots of companies are trying to sell you on this. At no point is an AI or robot dismissing your resume. Knock out questions are real – there are questions if you answer wrong then you are not moving onto the next round, right away. Visa questions and degrees are two very common knockout questions. Outside of that there IS NO AI THAT WILL DISMISS YOUR RESUME. It's a legal quagmire of equitable employment laws.

Who is looking at the resume? A human! We put together the resume for a human to read. Thus you format the resume in a certain way.

First filter: In the first 6/7 seconds that a recruiter looks at your resume, you HAVE to be one of the standouts that are the top 10%. They are looking for keywords that need to match, proof that you can do the role, things that are worth giving you more time on. You have to PROVE that you are a safe candidate.

Second filter: Phone screen w/ recruiter

Third filter: Technical phone screen / take home challenge / focused on coding

Fourth filter: onsite interview, coding interviews, hiring manager and system designs

Offer: congratulations!

The recruiter only has to find qualified/safe candidates for the hiring manager. The HIRING MANAGER is the one that they can bring into the company that will last the next 2/3 years.

“Have good communication skills. Have a past that you've woven into your narrative. Be able to communicate highly technical concepts in a digestable vernacular. Show excitement for the position. Follow up. Be able to show a history of consistency (anywhere).”

GETTING PAST THE FIRST FILTER:

What a recruiter sees: Name/Location/Current Role/Highest Education. This may present problems for somebody without a CS degree. Networking your way into the job lets you skip this first pass where the recruiter sees that you have no degree and/or your current job not being in software. This is the FIRST PASS. Thus it's very important to include college if you've gone to college and the first role on your resume SHOULD be software engineer. Talent is equally distributed but opportunity is not, thus there are things you have to do to get in the running.

In that first 6/7 seconds whether you're TECHNICAL, have IMPACT and LEADERSHIP capabilities. They need to be able to see that in 7 seconds and then the 20 seconds you get once you've piqued their interest.

Don't ever have words like 'learning', 'aspiring'

The language in terms of IMPACT and LEADERSHIP matter

Say they've started to look at your resume, they will start to fill in gaps. Sometimes for good, sometimes for bad. This is the gamble you can make in your resume. Two schools of thought: one be hyperexplicit or let them dream a little bit. Some ppl when they take a break to take care of family – they write 'family responsibilities'. Some employers see this, don't hold them against them, actually value the honesty, explained everything in detail and you move on. Some employers have a high workload and don't care, and don't move you on. HOWEVER this is a gamble since recruiters can fill this in negatively (lying, etc) or positively. Either be SUPER explicit or let the brain dance a bit.

Language matters (silent snitching): Little things matter when a human is reading it. “Helped and supported” vs “Led and Launched”. Think of how you feel when somebody says you 'led' the team or 'supported the new initative' vs 'launched the new initiative'. Sometimes people will use the wrong word even though they did the bulk of the work. Did you do most of the work – then you led the team! If you got it across the finish line then you launched the initiative!

Don't lie, just use the best possible words. You need to practice the proof, crafting the story and creating the narrative, creating the proof points in order to keep it consistent with your resume.

“A senior swe told me to always say I owned it and even exaggerate my work slightly , even if I got tons of help”

THE FORMAT TO USE IN YOUR RESUME: FOLLOW THE XYZ FORMAT! Accomplished by X as measured by Y by doing Z. The recruiter has to justify you are one of the 5/250 that moves onto the onsite screening. Most people forget one of these pieces.

X: What did you do? What was the impact that you had?

Y: How did you measure that impact?

Z: How did you make that come true? How did you actually do it? This is the thing that holds people the most, they don't explain the how. The HOW is the most important thing because that's what the recruiter is bragging about.

EXAMPLE:

OK: “Won second place in hackathon.”

Better: “Won second place out of 50 teams in hackathon.”

Best: “Won second place out of 50 teams in hackathon at NJ tech by working with two colleagues to develop an app that synchronizes mobile calenders.”

One sentence for each bullet point, ditch the fluff.

https://www.inc.com/bill-murphy-jr/google-recruiters-say-these-5-resume-tips-including-x-y-z-formula-will-improve-your-odds-of-getting-hired-at-google.html

If you're already working as an engineer, you should keep a dev log and keep it up to date each week, writing down the estimate impacts of your work.

/////////////////Resume template review//////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////

Two column resume works really well for career changers. It has a good summary (CONTROVERSIAL), typically only have a summary if you are senior/above OR you're a career changer. Originally included it because only senior ppl had summaries and it explained why we're switching careers. Maaaaybe a psychological play – maybe they weigh you as more technical off the riff.

Career changers are not valued highly in the marketplace if they are not ready to work right away → REALLY LEADING INTO THE PROOF. It's important to be a safe and competent candidate!

If you're applying to FAANG level companies, tier 1 startups, maybe two column might not be the best for you. There's some tradeoffs to have here.

Project section: What do projects make you think of? Who has projects? Entry level talent. If you have a project section, it might be a bit too much in the first six seconds because it has a smell. (Looking critically at the things at what might go in or be omitted from a resume).

The first bullet point has to be HIGH impact. Show TECHNICAL COMPETENCY, show LEADERSHIP.

“Strategy” = screaming HOW. HOW did you do it? HOW was it impactful?

/////////////////Second Resume//////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////

Listed experience in their time at 100Devs. (Their training program). Can plug in how much money you made from your client. Can put training program next to 100Devs, that's up to you.

Listed 100 hours project and (>100 hours).

Having more than one client can be very helpful because they can become extra bullet points.

Anything else you work on can be placed on your resume.

Adding volunteering/certifications is good but be sure not to add bootcampy/new dev stuff.

' "Secured a five-figure client contract for the 100Devs agency by working with a diverse engineering and business team to produce a website development proposal with detailed timeline, team composition, and project cost data information."

That was from participating in the colossal media campaign. What did it result in? A 100Devs contract.'

author: Michael Govaerts
title: michaelgovaerts/2024-06-21
html:
from HEMAD

here are some notes from tonight, june 21 2024. tune into the tea spill, i wont take notes for tha!

how to did practice and prep for your technical interview. every interview my peers that got were coding challenges and a white board PREP! Calms the nerves, this should be good 10-15min before interview,

I see many in the top 10/20 of the codewars clan, still without a job? Should we worry? Is it almost hopeless? no, codewars doesn't move the needle. Technically prep is just one door that you open. Dont put all your eggs in that basket, make sure you’re networking weekly! Dont just do the easy things!, a lot of folx are in that community but not in the hunt, keep that in mind,

I have been taken on board for a Hackathon sponsored by Google. The organizer said people will be head hunted after 4 days. How can I stand out as a participant best thing you could do: make sure you’re reaching out, you’re connecting with them afterward. Be persistent and follow up, take the initiative and things will eventually work in your favor,

I have weekly mock interviews with my senior dev friend. When its the real interviews I tend to feel nervous and anxious after being rejected many times.Tips? first: totally normal. Give yourself grace! It’s part of the process, just do more. With any company, any phone screen, get used to going into that environment, and once you’ve done some you’ll be used to it., know what you’re going into, actually ASK them what you’ll be tested on and more often than not, they'll tell you!, go into every interview with the mindset this person is your best friend and wants the best outta you. Just positive vibes for yourself to be even more confident!,

author: Michael Govaerts
title: michaelgovaerts/2024-06-29
html:
notes by sheep (Kayla)

1.I filled the 100 Maintainers form, but wasn't added to anything on the server. On June 18th you said mentioned about launching it. Is there space to join or not More people will be added slowly, making sure things are working and getting a starting direction for the maintainers, 2.Worried about upward mobility. Heard from several team members that manager doesn't fight for us. 2nd week on the job. Signed a two year contract. What do? Never eat alone. Fight for yourself, keep notes of what you’ve done, weekly journal of what you did and what it did for the company. Get your own network at work and people who want to fight for you. Always have a counter offer ready on promotions and raises.

author: Michael Govaerts
title: michaelgovaerts/2024-07-12
html:
How to Network Deep Dive with Jason E. Torres

by Hamza

Key Takeaways:

Know Your Strengths Understand and leverage your unique skills., Share your journey and projects on social media., Be responsive to messages., Aim to give more than you take.,

Engage in Conversations Schedule intentional coffee chats., Value your and others’ time.,

For Introverts: Focus on your “why” to help you step out of your comfort zone., Shared struggles can build connections., Join community spaces like Discord, Twitter Spaces, and tech forums.,

Building Connections: Networking is a vital life skill., Engage genuinely with people’s projects and interests., Aim to build friendships and relationships rather than just job opportunities.,

Jason’s Insights: Networking can significantly increase your chances of getting a job., Consistency and genuineness are crucial., Offer help and show up regularly without expecting immediate returns., Build connections organically rather than focusing on numbers.,

Quality Over Quantity: Focus on meaningful connections rather than the number of connections., Regularly engage with a small group that supports and shows up for you.,

Tracking Network: Jason doesn’t formally track his network to keep interactions genuine.,

General Advice: Networking doesn’t have to be limited to tech people., Being nice and approachable is more important than being important., Take your time and be helpful to others.,

Quotes and Community Feedback:

“Give, give, give, ask.” Helping others can indirectly help you solve your problems.,

Uncle Iroh: “Sometimes the best way to solve your own problems is to help someone else.” (edited)Friday, July 12, 2024 at 00:33

Practical Tips:

Post or show up consistently on social media and share your journey. {can just be replies, joining spaces}, Engage with familiar faces in community spaces., Use Twitter to share thoughts and connect with others., Be proactive in helping others, which will eventually pay off., Focus on building genuine relationships, not just increasing your follower count.,

Networking Strategy:

Start Small: Identify a few key people who consistently show up for you., Invest time and effort in these relationships.,

Be Consistent: Regularly engage with your network., Show genuine interest in their projects and successes.,

Stay Genuine: Keep interactions sincere and not just for personal gain., Avoid rushing the process; let relationships grow organically.,

By following these guidelines, you’ll build a strong, genuine network that supports your growth and helps you succeed in your career. (edited)Friday, July 12, 2024 at 00:33

author: Michael Govaerts
title: michaelgovaerts/2024-07-18
html:
notes by HEMAD

JULY 18 2024 SECRET HUDDLE NOTES Lesson 3 tech interviewer here, It's a 2.5hr JS interview. should I focus on practicing with Codewars and watching tutorial videos? Any video recs? pls help

Watch the js review class

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1sRJYuaqoiI&pp=ygUXMTAwZGV2cyBqcyBzdXBlciByZXZpZXc%3D

to learn the basics, yes practice codewars and take the shot! Learn from the interview process, improvements, changes. Beauti

How do I apply to do a tea spill?

Just send a modmail!

It's been my third attempt now in about a year and I'm struggling to keep up with the classes due to personal issues even when I've planned. Any advise please.

Give yourself grace. We’re all on our own journeys with different paces.

Build a plan that's realistic to your life. You need

What's your opinion on the current job market for Junior roles? Any advice regarding it? Ive been told itll take me 1-2 years to land a role due to the market.

Its going well, better than it has in a couple years. Check ?🎉celebrations channel!

1-2 years can be normal,it can vary for a lot of people. Some do it quicker, but the traditional route is about five years (uni/internship), If you get a job in 1-2 years you’ve achieved a shortcut!

layoffs are down, job postings are going up

The internship person here there are only 3 people in the company, it's a brand new startup is that bad?

Its not bad, just a very different experience. There may be NO process: no on-boarding, etc but you could learn a ton. It could go either way.

Just reached The Hunt in the program. Will there be a new hunt this October? Or should I just follow the huntober 2023 channel?

There will def be a new huntober, don't wait! Don't delay start today!

I'm at the hunt trying my best to get in touch with ppl in companies that I wanna join and even though I've tried to connect with many ppl I only got one chat

welcome to the game! If you’re using Linkedin connect, that could be a bad way. But that could be just me. My best platform is twitter and in person.

you should be sending dozens of messages over multiple platforms over spaced out time

Leon, you mentioned something about ADHD making us think we're boring in conversation, essentially self sabotaging. Can you elaborate more on that?

im not a doctor! Anecdotally, my experiences are my feelings on these things.

As for myself, folx with ADHD have trouble in convos because of the need to relate. Trying to connect with that person, but the connection is often the opposite of how you’re supposed to connect. Its just how my brain works. How to Win Friends and Influence People has really helped.

big advocate for therapy, talk to your doctor.

Mayanwolf: ADHD can make it difficult to listen actively without going off on a tangent or immediately trying to tie it to our own past experiences. I may have an offer at Salesforce. I have some developer friends saying it's a bad idea because it locks you in to their technology. Thoughts?

Working AT Salesforce is no different from joining any company. No worries there

Growth as an engineer should not come from your job for this very reason. You must always be training outside of your career to grow

I keep seeing that Software Development is a dying field that is going to be taken over by AI. What are your thoughts on this?

This is true, eventually and so will every other job. I don’t think its a quick as people think, maybe within ten years? By that point every major industry will be disrupted enough

At this point, theres no other field that you can learn for free and eventually get paid

if/when that happens you’ll have to move on anyway, take time now to learn

every company would still need people to integrate AI, be that person. Start/continue learning today

I've been networking hard in person the past few months but someone I networked with is a bit needy and an emotional vampire. How do I set boundaries? -You block them online. If in person, just say Hey i enjoyed this conversation, I’d like to take time to meet other folx and connect later! How do you respond to an interview offer after you were rejected for the job, that is, they contacted you to put you back into the running for the job? I've been going on some coffee chats.
